title: Discovering Bhupen Hazarika Setu
description: Also known as the Dhola-Sadiya Bridge, it is the longest bridge in India over water, spanning the mighty Lohit river.

title: Discovering Panimoor Falls
description: A fierce, roaring waterfall formed by the Kopili river, creating an awe-inspiring natural spectacle.

title: Discovering Kakochang Waterfalls
description: A magnificent waterfall cascading amidst tea gardens and rubber plantations near Kaziranga.

title: Discovering Enchey Monastery
description: An important seat of the Nyingma order, nestled on a ridge above Gangtok providing calm and spiritual solace.

title: Discovering Cholamu Lake
description: One of the highest lakes in the world, sitting just miles from the Tibet border with icy, crystal-clear waters.

title: Discovering Thangu Valley
description: A picturesque, high-altitude alpine valley blanketed with snow in winters and wildflowers in spring.

title: Discovering Goechala Trek
description: One of the most challenging and rewarding treks in India, leading right to the base of Mt. Kanchenjunga.

title: Discovering Kamala Sagar
description: An expansive artificial lake abutting a prominent Kali temple near the Bangladesh border.

title: Discovering Trishna Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A protected area particularly known for its sizeable population of Indian Gaur (bison) and diverse primates.

title: Discovering Pilak
description: An archaeological site holding 8th-12th century artifacts that signify the peaceful coexistence of Hinduism and Buddhism.

title: Discovering Chabimura
description: Famed for its massive, ancient rock carvings of Hindu deities on steep vertical walls along the Gomati River.

title: Discovering Murlen National Park
description: Often compared to the Amazon basin, this park has such dense canopies that sunlight rarely penetrates.

title: Discovering Solomon's Temple
description: A magnificent, sprawling church structure in Aizawl made of white marble, uniquely designed by a local sect.

title: Discovering Palak Dil
description: The largest lake in Mizoram, rich in biodiversity, legendary myths, and an ideal spot for bird watchers.

title: Discovering Tam Dil Lake
description: A serene natural lake surrounded by tropical forests, popular for relaxation and a local prawn hatchery.

title: Discovering Langthabal
description: A small hill adorned with relics of an old, historic palace and temples built by Maharaja Gambhir Singh.

title: Discovering Willong Khullen
description: Often called the Stonehenge of Manipur, featuring hundreds of mysterious, tall, erected stone monoliths.

title: Discovering Singda Dam
description: The highest mud dam in the world, offering serene lake waters and surrounding green hills perfect for picnics.

title: Discovering Moreh
description: A bustling commercial town on the Indo-Myanmar border, acting as a melting pot of cultures and trade.

title: Discovering Chuchuyimlang
description: An Ao Naga village famous for its grand celebrations of the Moatsu festival and scenic hilltop views.

title: Discovering Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A remote sanctuary housing rare species like the tragopan pheasant, slender loris, and leopards.

title: Discovering Mount Pauna
description: The third highest peak in Nagaland, providing excellent trekking trails and spectacular views of the Himalayas.

title: Discovering Dzuleke
description: A pristine, eco-friendly village offering a secluded retreat amidst dense forests and clear streams.

title: Discovering Jakrem Hot Springs
description: Famous for its hot sulphur water springs, believed to possess curative medicinal properties.

title: Discovering Kyllang Rock
description: A massive, dome-shaped granite rock offering panoramic views of the surrounding Khasi hills.

title: Discovering Don Bosco Museum
description: A major cultural center in Shillong providing a comprehensive overview of the indigenous cultures of Northeast India.

title: Discovering Ward's Lake
description: An artificial lake surrounded by a lush botanical garden and cobblestone pathways in the heart of Shillong.

title: Discovering Malinithan
description: An archaeological site containing ruins of a Hindu temple dating back to the 14th century.

title: Discovering Mayodia Pass
description: A breathtaking high-altitude pass near Roing, famous for heavy snowfall and pristine white landscapes.

title: Discovering Dong Village
description: The easternmost village in India, renowned as the place that receives the very first rays of the sun in the country.

title: Discovering Namsai Golden Pagoda
description: A magnificent Burmese-style Buddhist temple set amidst beautifully landscaped gardens.

title: Discovering Sonapur Tea Estate
description: Located just outside Guwahati, providing a quick, refreshing getaway into lush greenery and tea production.

title: Discovering Margherita
description: Famous for its historic coal mines, plywood factories, and surrounding sprawling tea gardens.

title: Discovering Harmutty Tea Estate
description: A highly scenic tea estate framed by the backdrop of the Dikrong river and the Arunachal hills.

title: Discovering Corramore Tea Estate
description: Founded by Robert Logan, offering spectacular views of the Kaziranga ranges and premium tea blends.

title: Discovering Halmari Tea Estate
description: A prestigious tea garden renowned globally for producing some of the highest quality orthodox Assam teas.

title: Discovering Monabarie Tea Estate
description: Recognized as the largest tea estate in Asia, featuring endless rolling plains of pristine tea bushes.

title: Discovering Tocklai Tea Research Institute
description: One of the oldest and largest tea research stations in the world, delving into the science of tea.

title: Discovering Temi Tea Garden
description: The only tea estate in Sikkim, celebrated for its organic tea and breathtaking views of Mt. Kanchenjunga.

title: Discovering Jorhat Tea Estates
description: Known as the Tea Capital of the World, offering lush green estates and colonial-era bungalows.

title: Discovering Longleng
description: A rugged and enchanting district in Nagaland, home to the Phom Nagas and their unique traditions.

title: Discovering Daporijo
description: A quaint town surrounded by green hills, caves, and suspension bridges made of bamboo and cane.

title: Discovering Tuting
description: An offbeat paradise in Arunachal Pradesh offering extreme white water rafting on the Siang River.

title: Discovering Barsey Rhododendron Sanctuary
description: A spectacular sanctuary in Sikkim that transforms into a vibrant canvas of blooming rhododendrons in spring.

title: Discovering Yuksom
description: The first capital of Sikkim, an important historical town that serves as the basecamp for the Dzongri trek.

title: Discovering Khecheopalri Lake
description: A sacred, wish-fulfilling lake heavily venerated by Buddhists and Hindus, naturally hidden in a dense forest.

title: Discovering Rumtek Monastery
description: One of the largest and most significant Tibetan Buddhist monasteries in Sikkim, rich in murals and artifacts.

title: Discovering Zuluk
description: A rugged, high-altitude hamlet in East Sikkim, known for the dizzying loops of the Old Silk Route.

title: Discovering Tripura Sundari Temple
description: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas, a deeply revered ancient temple built by Maharaja Dhanya Manikya.

title: Discovering Jampui Hills
description: The highest hill range in Tripura, famous for its eternal spring-like climate and lush orange orchards.

title: Discovering Sepahijala Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A biodiversity hotspot known for its clouded leopards, spectacled monkeys, and a large botanical garden.

title: Discovering Ujjayanta Palace
description: A magnificent royal palace in Agartala, now serving as the state museum of Tripura.

title: Discovering Aizawl
description: The picturesque capital of Mizoram, beautifully perched on ridges overlooking the Tlawng river valley.

title: Discovering Dampa Tiger Reserve
description: The largest wildlife sanctuary in Mizoram, featuring dense canopies and diverse endangered species.

title: Discovering Phawngpui (Blue Mountain)
description: The highest peak in Mizoram, known for orchids, rhododendrons, and sweeping views of the blue-tinted hills.

title: Discovering Tharon Cave
description: A historically significant, deep cave network in Tamenglong, offering an exciting spelunking experience.

title: Discovering Ima Keithel
description: Asia's largest all-women market, a massive bustling hub of commerce and Manipuri tradition.

title: Discovering Keibul Lamjao National Park
description: The only floating national park in the world, home to the endangered Sangai deer.

title: Discovering Imphal War Cemetery
description: A well-maintained memorial honoring British and Indian soldiers who fell during the Battle of Imphal.

title: Discovering Benreu
description: An unspoiled village of the Zeliang tribe, acting as a living museum of primitive Naga culture.

title: Discovering Mount Saramati
description: The highest peak in Nagaland, located near the Myanmar border, offering a challenging alpine trek.

title: Discovering Kachari Ruins
description: Mushroom-domed pillars and ruins dating back to the 10th century Kachari civilization in Dimapur.

title: Discovering Siju Cave
description: Known as the Bat Cave, it features impressive limestone stalactites, stalagmites, and deep river passages.

title: Discovering Nohkalikai Falls
description: The tallest plunge waterfall in India, dropping spectacularly into a deep green pool below.

title: Discovering Nartiang Monoliths
description: A massive collection of ancient megalithic stones erected to honor the old Jaintia kings.

title: Discovering Mawphlang Sacred Grove
description: An ancient, deeply revered forest preserved by local tribes, rich in rare medicinal plants.

title: Discovering Elephant Falls
description: A popular three-tiered waterfall located just outside Shillong, accessible via scenic staircases.

title: Discovering Shillong Peak
description: The highest point in Meghalaya, offering a breathtaking panoramic view of the entire city and waterfalls.

title: Discovering Pakke Tiger Reserve
description: A haven for hornbills and tigers, celebrated for its successful community-driven conservation models.

title: Discovering Anini
description: A remote, misty town offering pristine landscapes, deep valleys, and raw natural beauty.

title: Discovering Aalo
description: A scenic town situated at the confluence of Sipu and Yomgo rivers, known for its orange orchards.

title: Discovering Nuranang Falls
description: A stunning and relatively undiscovered 100-meter high waterfall located in the Tawang district.

title: Discovering Itanagar
description: The capital of Arunachal Pradesh, home to the historical Ita Fort and vibrant tribal culture.

title: Discovering Mayong
description: A mysterious village in Assam historically famous as the cradle of black magic and sorcery.

title: Discovering Tezpur
description: The City of Eternal Romance, rich in ancient mythology, ruins, and scenic parks along the Brahmaputra.

title: Discovering Dehing Patkai National Park
description: Known as the Amazon of the East, it is the only rainforest in Assam with rich flora and fauna.

title: Discovering Sualkuchi
description: The textile center of Assam, famous for producing high-quality Muga and Pat silk.

title: Discovering Digboi
description: The Oil City of Assam, featuring the oldest operating oil well and refinery in Asia.

title: Discovering Umananda Island
description: The smallest inhabited river island in the world, known for its Shiva temple and golden langurs.

title: Discovering Hajo
description: An ancient pilgrimage center for three religions: Hinduism, Buddhism, and Islam.

title: Discovering Neermahal
description: A stunning water palace built in the middle of Rudrasagar Lake, showcasing Hindu and Islamic architecture.

title: Discovering Unakoti
description: An ancient pilgrimage center with massive rock-cut sculptures and stone images of Hindu deities.

title: Discovering Vantawng Falls
description: The highest uninterrupted waterfall in Mizoram, surrounded by thick, lush bamboo forests.

title: Discovering Champhai
description: A vibrant commercial town known as the Rice Bowl of Mizoram, offering stunning hilly terrain.

title: Discovering Reiek Tlang
description: A picturesque hill offering panoramic views of Aizawl and a model Mizo heritage village.

title: Discovering Khongjom War Memorial
description: A tribute to the martyrs of the historic Anglo-Manipuri War of 1891.

title: Discovering Andro Village
description: An ancient village known for its unique traditional pottery and the cultural heritage complex.

title: Discovering Shirui Hills
description: Famous for the rare Shirui Lily flower which blooms only on the peak of this hill.

title: Discovering Kangla Fort
description: The old palace of the Kingdom of Manipur, rich in history, archaeology, and local mythology.

title: Discovering Loktak Lake
description: The largest freshwater lake in Northeast India, famous for its circular floating phumdis.

title: Discovering Namchi
description: The cultural capital of Sikkim featuring replicas of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites.

title: Discovering Ravangla
description: Known for the magnificent Buddha Park and undisturbed views of the Himalayas.

title: Discovering Gurudongmar Lake
description: One of the highest lakes in the world, considered sacred by Buddhists, Sikhs, and Hindus.

title: Discovering Yumthang Valley
description: Also known as the Valley of Flowers of the East, surrounded by snow-capped mountains.

title: Discovering Lachen
description: A base camp for Gurudongmar Lake, offering rugged terrain and pristine alpine views.

title: Discovering Lachung
description: A picturesque mountain village in North Sikkim serving as the base for Yumthang Valley.

title: Discovering Pelling
description: A serene town providing the closest and most breathtaking views of the Kanchenjunga range.

title: Discovering Nathula Pass
description: A mountain pass on the Indo-China border that was once a part of the ancient Silk Route.

title: Discovering Tsomgo Lake
description: A spectacular glacial lake situated at a high altitude, changing colors with the seasons.

title: Discovering Gangtok
description: The capital of Sikkim, offering stunning views of Mt. Kanchenjunga and vibrant Tibetan culture.

title: Discovering Nokrek National Park
description: A UNESCO biosphere reserve protecting the red panda and wild Asian elephants.

title: Discovering Balpakram National Park
description: A pristine national park in the Garo Hills, known for its deep gorge and rich biodiversity.

title: Discovering Krag Suri Falls
description: One of the most aesthetically pleasing waterfalls in Meghalaya, known for its deep blue pool.

title: Discovering Mawsynram
description: Currently holding the record for the highest average rainfall globally, featuring lush green landscapes.

title: Discovering Umiam Lake
description: A sprawling man-made reservoir surrounded by sylvan hills, acting as a gateway to Shillong.

title: Discovering Laitlum Canyon
description: Translating to 'End of the Hills', this canyon offers dramatic, sweeping views of misty valleys.

title: Discovering Nongriat Double Decker Root Bridge
description: A spectacular natural wonder requiring a challenging hike to view bridges formed entirely of tree roots.

title: Discovering Mawlynnong
description: Dubbed Asia's cleanest village, it is a model for eco-friendly living and community-driven sanitation.

title: Discovering Dawki
description: A border town acclaimed for the crystal-clear Umngot River where boats seem to float on air.

title: Discovering Cherrapunji (Sohra)
description: Historic wettest place on earth, famous for dramatic waterfalls, cliffs, and living root bridges.

title: Discovering Pfutsero
description: The highest altitude town in Nagaland, known for its cold weather and stunning cherry blossoms.

title: Discovering Wokha
description: The land of the Lotha tribe and famous for the Amur Falcon roosting site near Doyang Reservoir.

title: Discovering Mon
description: The land of the Konyak Nagas, famous for their tattooed faces and traditional wood carving.

title: Discovering Tuophema Village
description: A tourist village offering an authentic Naga lifestyle experience in traditional huts.

title: Discovering Mokokchung
description: The cultural and intellectual center of the Ao Naga tribe, offering scenic views and rich traditions.

title: Discovering Japfu Peak
description: The second-highest peak in Nagaland offering panoramic views of the Dzukou Valley.

title: Discovering Khonoma Village
description: India's first green village, known for its terraced agriculture and deep-rooted conservation efforts.

title: Discovering Kohima War Cemetery
description: A memorial dedicated to soldiers of the Allied Forces who died in the Second World War at Kohima.

title: Discovering Kisama Heritage Village
description: The venue for the famous Hornbill Festival, designed to protect and promote traditional Naga culture.

title: Discovering Dzukou Valley
description: Famous for its rolling hills, seasonal wildflowers, and incredibly scenic trekking trails.

title: Discovering Bhalukpong
description: A beautiful town bordering Assam, known for the Kameng River and orchid reserves.

title: Discovering Pasighat
description: The oldest town of Arunachal Pradesh, situated on the banks of the mighty Siang River.

title: Discovering Roing
description: Known for the serene Mehao Lake and the ancient Bhismaknagar Fort.

title: Discovering Mechuka
description: An offbeat destination known for its pristine beauty, snow-capped peaks, and the Siyom River.

title: Discovering Dirang
description: A charming valley town famous for its hot water springs and ancient dzongs.

title: Discovering Sela Pass
description: A high-altitude mountain pass providing spectacular views and serving as the gateway to Tawang.

title: Discovering Bomdila
description: Offers brilliant landscape views of the snow-clad Himalayas alongside sprawling apple orchards.

title: Discovering Namdapha National Park
description: A biodiversity hotspot and tiger reserve with diverse ecosystems ranging from tropical to alpine.

title: Discovering Ziro Valley
description: A picturesque valley known for its lush pine hills, terraced rice fields, and the indigenous Apatani tribe.

title: Discovering Tawang
description: Famous for the largest Buddhist monastery in India and breathtaking high-altitude landscapes.

title: Discovering Orang National Park
description: Often called mini Kaziranga, known for rhinos, tigers, and a rich variety of fish.

title: Discovering Sivasagar
description: The former capital of the Ahom Kingdom, featuring magnificent ancient palaces and temples.

title: Discovering Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Known for having the highest density of one-horned rhinoceroses in the world.

title: Discovering Nameri National Park
description: Nestled in the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas, known for its elephants and thrilling river activities.

title: Discovering Dibru-Saikhowa National Park
description: A national park renowned for its feral horses and vast wetlands.

title: Discovering Haflong
description: Assam's only hill station, characterized by rolling mountains, lush green valleys, and pristine lakes.

title: Discovering Kamakhya Temple
description: One of the oldest of the 51 Shakti Pithas, located on the Nilachal Hill in Guwahati.

title: Discovering Manas National Park
description: A biosphere reserve and UNESCO Heritage site offering dense jungles and sightings of tigers and elephants.

title: Discovering Kaziranga National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for sheltering two-thirds of the world's great one-horned rhinoceroses.

title: Discovering Majuli
description: Majuli is the world's largest river island known for its eco-friendly lifestyle and rich Assamese culture.

title: The Shining White Palace of Agartala
description: Located in the heart of the capital city, Ujjayanta Palace is a magnificent former royal palace that now serves as the State Museum. Built by Maharaja Radha Kishore Manikya in 1901, the palace features neo-classical architecture, three large domes, and extensive Mughal-style gardens with fountains. It houses a vast collection of artifacts that showcase the rich history, culture, and arts of Northeast India.

title: The Lost Hill of One Less Than a Crore
description: Unakoti is a spectacular heritage site featuring massive rock-cut sculptures and stone images of Hindu deities dating back to the 7th–9th centuries. The name "Unakoti" means "one less than a crore" (ten million) in Bengali. The central attraction is the 30-foot-high head of Lord Shiva, known as Unakotiswara Kal Bhairava. These ancient carvings are nestled in a lush, green forest setting with waterfalls cascading over the rocks.

title: The Lake Palace of Eastern India
description: Neermahal, meaning "Water Palace," is a former royal palace built by Maharaja Bir Bikram Kishore Manikya Bahadur in the middle of Rudrasagar Lake. It is a stunning blend of Hindu and Islamic architectural styles and is the largest of its kind in India. The palace was designed as a summer resort and looks particularly magical when illuminated at night, reflecting its white and red towers in the calm lake waters.

title: High-Altitude Cultural Heritage and Views
description: Reiek Peak is a majestic mountain offering some of the most spectacular 360-degree views of the Mizo hills and surrounding valleys. At its base, the peak features a meticulously created traditional Mizo Heritage Village. This living museum showcases authentic replicas of different Mizo tribal huts ('Morungs') and artifacts. It serves as a vital hub for preserving Mizo culture, often hosting traditional dances and festivals against the backdrop of the rugged Himalayan foothills.

title: Asia's Model Clean Village
description: While often associated with Meghalaya, Mizoram’s border includes access to this incredible cultural marvel. Mawlynnong is world-famous for holding the model distinction of being 'Asia's Cleanest Village.' It is a picturesque Khasi and Mizo settlement where cleanliness is a deeply ingrained way of life. Visitors are charmed by bamboo dustbins lining paved pathways, immaculate flower gardens, and beautiful traditional stilt houses. The community practices highly sustainable living and offers an intimate look into traditional hospitality and eco-friendly culture.

title: Asia's Model Clean Village
description: While often associated with Meghalaya, Mizoram’s border includes access to this incredible cultural marvel. Mawlynnong is world-famous for holding the model distinction of being 'Asia's Cleanest Village.' It is a picturesque Khasi and Mizo settlement where cleanliness is a deeply ingrained way of life. Visitors are charmed by bamboo dustbins lining paved pathways, immaculate flower gardens, and beautiful traditional stilt houses. The community practices highly sustainable living and offers an intimate look into traditional hospitality and eco-friendly culture.

title: Asia's Model Clean Village
description: While often associated with Meghalaya, Mizoram’s border includes access to this incredible cultural marvel. Mawlynnong is world-famous for holding the model distinction of being 'Asia's Cleanest Village.' It is a picturesque Khasi and Mizo settlement where cleanliness is a deeply ingrained way of life. Visitors are charmed by bamboo dustbins lining paved pathways, immaculate flower gardens, and beautiful traditional stilt houses. The community practices highly sustainable living and offers an intimate look into traditional hospitality and eco-friendly culture.

title: Mizoram’s Highest Waterfall
description: Vantawng Falls is the highest waterfall in Mizoram, cascading from a height of over 750 feet. It is a stunning natural spectacle located in the Serchhip district, hidden deep within dense bamboo groves and thick tropical forests. The fall is composed of two tiers, creating a dramatic, powerful plume of water that crashes into a turquoise pool. The surrounding lush, undisturbed jungle canyon makes it an excellent location for nature photography and trekking.

title: Breathtaking Blue Waters of Jaintia Hills
description: Krang Suri is arguably one of the most beautiful waterfalls in India. Hidden deep within the Jaintia Hills, it is famous for its magical, crystal-clear turquoise blue water. The falls cascade over a natural limestone cliff into a massive, shimmering pool. A paved path leads visitors down a scenic jungle trek to this hidden paradise, which is perfect for swimming and relaxing.

activities: boating on the placid river above the falls.

title: Discovering Asia's Cleanest Village
description: Mawlynnong is famous for holding the distinction of being 'Asia's Cleanest Village.' It is a picturesque Khasi settlement where cleanliness is a way of life, not just a rule. Visitors are charmed by bamboo dustbins lining paved pathways, immaculate flower gardens, and beautiful traditional stilt houses. The community practices sustainable living and offers a look into traditional Khasi hospitality and culture.

activities: visiting the 'Sky Walk' (bamboo structure for border views)

title: Discovering Asia's Cleanest Village
description: Mawlynnong is famous for holding the distinction of being 'Asia's Cleanest Village.' It is a picturesque Khasi settlement where cleanliness is a way of life, not just a rule. Visitors are charmed by bamboo dustbins lining paved pathways, immaculate flower gardens, and beautiful traditional stilt houses. The community practices sustainable living and offers a look into traditional Khasi hospitality and culture.

activities: visiting the 'Sky Walk' (bamboo structure for border views)

title: Walking on Ancient Living Structures
description: Nongriat Village is world-famous for its spectacular 'Living Root Bridges,' including the incredible 'Double Decker' bridge. These functional masterpieces are meticulously grown and woven from the strong roots of the Ficus elastica tree over centuries by the local Khasi people. They are a profound example of sustainable architecture in harmony with nature, deep within dense, sub-tropical jungle.

title: The Home of the Shirui Lily
description: Located in Ukhrul, this peak is the only place in the world where the rare and beautiful Shirui Lily (Lilium mackliniae) grows naturally. The peak offers breathtaking 360-degree views of the surrounding hills, especially during the blooming season in May and June.

title: The Market Run Solely by Women
description: Ima Keithel is a unique 500-year-old market in Imphal managed entirely by thousands of married women, making it Asia's (and possibly the world’s) largest all-women's market. It is a bustling center of commerce where everything from handlooms to fresh local produce is sold.

title: The World’s Only Floating National Park
description: Loktak Lake is the largest freshwater lake in Northeast India, famous for its unique "Phumdis"—naturally formed floating islands of vegetation, soil, and organic matter. It is home to Keibul Lamjao, the world’s only floating national park and the last natural habitat of the endangered Sangai (Brow-antlered deer).

title: Festival of Festivals
description: Held annually at the Kisama Heritage Village, the Hornbill Festival is a vibrant celebration of the diverse Naga tribes' culture, music, dance, and food. The festival venue itself features traditional tribal 'Morungs' (male dormitories) with elaborate wood carvings and architecture. It is a powerful display of unity, tradition, and artistic expression.

title: Green Village and Warrior History
description: Khonoma is India's first "Green Village," renowned for its pioneering conservation efforts, unique alder timber forestry, and traditional stone terraces. It is a historical village of the Angami Naga tribe, famous for its resistance against British colonial expansion and its beautiful landscape of terraced fields and traditional stilt houses.

title: Exploring the Mystical Lily Valley
description: Known as the "Valley of Flowers of the East," Dzüko is a breathtaking high-altitude valley famous for its vibrant natural beauty, rolling green hills, winding streams, and the rare Dzüko Lily that blooms in monsoon. It offers excellent trekking opportunities with pristine environmental landscapes.

title: The High Altitude Gateway to Tawang
description: Sitting at an elevation of 13,700 feet, Sela Pass is a breathtaking mountain pass that remains snow-covered most of the year. It features the crystal-clear Sela Lake (Paradise Lake) and offers panoramic views of the rugged Himalayan peaks.

title: The Serene Landscapes of Ziro
description: A stunning high-altitude valley home to the Apatani tribe. It is famous for its unique paddy-cum-fish cultivation, pine-clad hills, and the annual Ziro Festival of Music. The valley is a UNESCO World Heritage Tentative Site known for its sustainable lifestyle and natural beauty.

title: Spiritual Heritage of the Himalayas
description: The largest monastery in India and the second largest in the world, Tawang Monastery sits at 10,000 feet. It is a stunning example of Tibetan Buddhist architecture, housing a massive gilded Buddha statue and a priceless collection of ancient scriptures.

title: Pilgrimage to the Nilachal Hills
description: One of the oldest and most revered Shakti Peethas in India, dedicated to the Goddess Kamakhya. The temple features unique medieval architecture with a beehive-shaped dome and is the center of the famous Ambubachi Mela.

title: Bohag Bihu Festival
description: The Assamese New Year and the most significant harvest festival. The state comes alive with traditional folk dances, rhythmic dhol beats, and local delicacies like Pitha.

title: Exploring the World's Largest River Island
description: Nestled in the Brahmaputra River, Majuli is the hub of Assamese neo-Vaishnavite culture. It is known for its traditional "Satras" (monasteries) and unique mask-making heritage.

title: Wildlife Safari in Kaziranga
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site famous for being the primary home of the Great Indian One-Horned Rhinoceros. It features a diverse ecosystem of tall elephant grass and marshland.
